title: lathyrus latifolius var. ensifolius
intro: Lathyrus latifolius var. ensifolius, also known as the broad-leaved pea, is a perennial legume with broad, flat leaves that belongs to the pea family. The plant contains a compound called ODAP, which is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The broad-leaved pea has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
